WAEC_English_Language_Objective_Question.title: WAEC English Language Objective Test Questions (Complete Merged List)
WAEC_English_Language_Objective_Question.instruction: The following is a complete list of the WAEC English Language objective questions and options you provided, organized into sections with sequential numbering (1-82), including the best fit answer and a brief explanation.

questions.question: Abdul’s contribution at the meeting was seen by many as **invaluable**, but the chairman said it was ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'invaluable'** (extremely useful/precious) is **'useless'**.

questions.question: The boy may be **culpable**, but his brother is surely ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'culpable'** (deserving blame) is **'innocent'**.

questions.question: Our class teacher used to be **rigid** in his opinions but he is now ____ of other people’s views.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'rigid'** (unwilling to change) is **'tolerant'**.

questions.question: Some workers were quite **affable** at the meeting but their leader was rather ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'affable'** (friendly, good-natured) is **'antagonistic'** (hostile).

questions.question: The driver believed he would be **castigated** by all the passengers but he was ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'castigated'** (severely criticized/punished) is **'commended'** (praised).

questions.question: The manager was **eulogized** for his speech while his assistant was ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'eulogized'** (praised highly) is **'denounced'** (publicly condemned or criticized).

questions.question: Some people are **generous** towards strangers, while others are _____ towards them.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'generous'** is **'mean'** (unwilling to give or share).

questions.question: The baby who had all along been **calm** suddenly became ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'calm'** is **'restless'**.

questions.question: While Ade is **conceited**, his wife is quite self-effacing. (Implied Antonym Question)
questions.explanation: The sentence itself provides the antonym: **'self-effacing'** (modest) is the opposite of **'conceited'** (vain). The options provided are not used to fill a gap in this specific sentence structure.

questions.question: Bakary is a **prudent** husband while Musa is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of **'prudent'** (acting with care for the future, economical) is **'extravagant'** (lacking restraint in spending money).

questions.question: The incumbent chairman won the election by a _____ of twenty votes.
questions.explanation: **'Margin'** is the standard term for the difference in votes.

questions.question: Since all items on the agenda had been discussed, the meeting was ____.
questions.explanation: **'Adjourned'** means to break off a meeting, often with the intent to resume later or end formally.

questions.question: The police explained that all the aggrieved persons should seek _____ in the court of law.
questions.explanation: **'Redress'** means remedy or compensation for a wrong or grievance.

questions.question: Our principal has a large _____ of cows.
questions.explanation: **'Herd'** is the collective noun for cows.

questions.question: The first storey building in Nigeria stands as a historic ____.
questions.explanation: A **'monument'** is a structure erected to commemorate a person or event.

questions.question: I returned from the vacation refreshed and ____.
questions.explanation: **'Rejuvenated'** means to make (someone) look or feel younger, fresher, or more vigorous.

questions.question: She was advised to see a _____ because she had an unusual skin disease.
questions.explanation: A **'dermatologist'** is a doctor specializing in conditions of the skin.

questions.question: The doctor’s _____ was that the patient was suffering from cancer.
questions.explanation: A **'diagnosis'** is the identification of the nature of an illness.

questions.question: Tom’s father gave him a valuable gift as an _____ to make him work harder in school.
questions.explanation: An **'incentive'** is a thing that motivates or encourages one to do something.

questions.question: We have not yet heard the weather ____.
questions.explanation: **'Forecast'** is the term for a prediction of future weather conditions.

questions.question: Ojo was cautioned not to **jangle the old man’s nerve**. This means that Ojo was cautioned not to ____.
questions.explanation: To **'jangle one's nerves'** means to irritate or **annoy** intensely.

questions.question: Amina was completely **swept off her feet** the first time she met Gabriel. This means that she ____.
questions.explanation: To be **'swept off one's feet'** means to be overwhelmed by infatuation, usually by falling deeply in love.

questions.question: My trust in Abah suddenly began to **wane**.
questions.explanation: To **'wane'** means to **decrease** in vigor, power, or extent.

questions.question: Some believe it is **ominous** for the owl to perch on the roof of a house.
questions.explanation: **'Ominous'** means suggesting that something bad is going to happen, synonymous with **'portentous'**.

questions.question: The new shopping mall is on the **fringe** of the town.
questions.explanation: The **'fringe'** of something is its **edge** or border.

questions.question: The supervisor **spurned** the offer of help from the cleaner.
questions.explanation: To **'spurn'** means to reject with disdain or contempt, synonymous with **'rebuffed'**.

questions.question: I have **eventually** found the book.
questions.explanation: **'Eventually'** means in the end, especially after a long delay, synonymous with **'finally'**.

questions.question: His response showed that he **kept abreast of** events in the country.
questions.explanation: To **'keep abreast of'** means to stay **well-informed about**.

questions.question: Kofi is rather **audacious**; he jumps off high fences.
questions.explanation: **'Audacious'** means showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks, synonymous with **'daring'**.

questions.question: The criminal approached the crowd **menacingly**.
questions.explanation: **'Menacingly'** means suggesting the presence of danger, synonymous with **'threateningly'**.

questions.question: The new shopping plaza is **easy on the eye**.
questions.explanation: **'Easy on the eye'** is an idiom meaning **attractive** or pleasant to look at.

questions.question: That **nonchalant** attitude of Jonah’s ____ me.
questions.explanation: A **'nonchalant'** (casually unconcerned) attitude is most likely to **irritate** someone who is concerned.

questions.question: Our school is the **last but one** on that road.
questions.explanation: **'Last but one'** is an expression meaning **the one before the last**.

questions.question: He said that they _____ painting the house by then.
questions.explanation: This is reported speech. The future perfect ('will have finished') shifts to the conditional perfect **('would have finished')**.

questions.question: I will wear my _____ dress to the dance.
questions.explanation: The correct order of adjectives is Age-Color-Material: **'new blue silk'**.

questions.question: If I _____ an eagle, I would fly to distant lands.
questions.explanation: In unreal conditional sentences (Type 2), **'were'** is used for all persons ('If I were...').

questions.question: The teacher needs _____ to write the note on the chalkboard.
questions.explanation: **'Chalk'** is generally an uncountable noun, so **'some chalk'** is correct.

questions.question: The man said that he _____ eaten there the previous day.
questions.explanation: Reported speech shifts the past simple/present perfect to the past perfect **('had eaten')**.

questions.question: Taya was **beaten at his own game**. This means that:

options.D: Taya’s methods were used to defeat him

questions.explanation: To be **'beaten at one's own game'** means one's methods were used to defeat them.

questions.question: We never believed that Father would **pull through** that fever. This means that:
questions.explanation: To **'pull through'** means to **recover from the sickness** or survive a crisis.

questions.question: To be healthy, we need to eat a _____ diet every day.
questions.explanation: The correct term in nutrition is a **'balanced'** diet.

questions.question: I can rustle _____ an omelette at short notice.
questions.explanation: To **'rustle up'** is an idiom meaning to quickly prepare food.

questions.question: Banku was least ___ unhappy after the fight.
questions.explanation: This phrasing is awkward, but **'much'** is the only intensifier that could grammatically follow 'least' in some non-standard contexts ('least much' meaning not at all/minimally). The question is likely flawed.

questions.question: Everything would be ___ cheaper in the sales.
questions.explanation: **'Considerably'** is the most appropriate adverb to indicate a large degree of cheapness.

questions.question: The boot of my new car was ___ in the accident.
questions.explanation: **'Dented'** is the specific term for an impact mark on a metal surface like a car boot.

questions.question: Parents should create an environment ___ their children can grow.
questions.explanation: **'In which'** or 'where' is the correct relative pronoun phrase.

questions.question: In spite of their shortcomings, we admire them. (Select the connecting word)
questions.explanation: The sentence is already grammatically complete. None of the options fit the existing structure. It likely required a rewrite using one of the connectors.

questions.question: Amadi's plan ___ at the last moment.
questions.explanation: To **'fall through'** means to fail or come to nothing.

questions.question: You think I am hungry, ___?
questions.explanation: The tag question refers to the main verb/clause, **'You think'**, making the tag **'don't you'**.

questions.question: Maria ___ tears when she was told that she failed the final examination.
questions.explanation: To **'broke into tears'** is the correct idiom meaning to suddenly start crying.

questions.question: Jide ___ before I arrived.
questions.explanation: The Past Perfect **('had eaten')** is needed to show an action completed before another past action ('arrived').

questions.question: He claimed to be an expert, but he knew next to ___ about it.
questions.explanation: **'Next to nothing'** is an idiom meaning almost nothing.

questions.question: Deficiency ___ just one essential vitamin may cause illness.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is **'in'**: 'deficiency **in** (something)'.

questions.question: This is the ___ he has submitted his assignment since joining the class.
questions.explanation: The superlative **'earliest'** means the first or soonest time.

questions.question: It is wise to ___ with what one has rather than go borrowing.
questions.explanation: To **'make do'** is an idiom meaning to manage with what is available.

questions.question: The teacher was hasty in meting out punishment. (Synonym for 'hasty' required, but options are adverbs)
questions.explanation: The question asks for a synonym of 'hasty,' but the options provided are adverbs of degree/manner. This question is flawed.

questions.question: Everybody should treat their parents with respect, ___?
questions.explanation: The tag question for a statement using **'Everybody/Everyone'** is **'shouldn't they'**.

questions.question: The experienced clerk found herself at ___.
questions.explanation: **'At a crossroads'** is the correct idiom, meaning at a point of decision.

questions.question: We are playing ___ the problems of the company.
questions.explanation: To **'play down'** means to minimize or make seem less important.

questions.question: Did you notice that the ___ had been removed?
questions.explanation: **'Price tag'** is the correct compound noun.

questions.question: One has to be careful of ___.
questions.explanation: When the indefinite pronoun **'one'** is the subject, its possessive form is **'one's'**.

questions.question: It is mandatory that consumables should have ___.
questions.explanation: This question is incomplete or flawed as none of the options logically complete the sentence (e.g., should have an 'expiry date').

questions.question: passage:
questions.explanation: **'Passage'** and **'message'** rhyme.

questions.question: coarse:
questions.explanation: **'Coarse'** and **'chore'** rhyme in non-rhotic accents.

questions.question: dumb:
questions.explanation: **'Dumb'** does not rhyme with 'comb'. This item is likely flawed (it should rhyme with 'come' or 'thumb').

questions.question: wolf:
questions.explanation: **'Wolf'** and **'wood'** share a similar short /ʊ/ vowel sound, making it the best fit among the options.

questions.question: muscle:
questions.explanation: **'Muscle'** and **'puzzle'** rhyme.

questions.question: trouser:
questions.explanation: **'Trouser'** and **'browser'** rhyme.

questions.question: aisle:
questions.explanation: **'Aisle'** and **'isle'** are homophones and rhyme perfectly.

questions.question: On the day of the election, the voters will go to the polling ___.
questions.explanation: Voters go to the **polling station** (the location).

questions.question: The winner will be ___.
questions.explanation: In an election, the winner is **chosen** (selected by vote).

questions.question: The local government chairman's period/reign would soon ___.
questions.explanation: A fixed term of office is said to **expire**.

questions.question: This will commence with the selection of ___.
questions.explanation: The electoral process begins with selecting **candidates**.

questions.question: Political parties will present their ___ to the public to inform them of their intentions.
questions.explanation: A **manifesto** is a public declaration of policy.

questions.question: The process of ___ for votes has a timeframe.
questions.explanation: **Canvassing** is the process of soliciting votes.

questions.question: Voting is usually done by universal ___.
questions.explanation: **Universal suffrage** is the right of all adult citizens to vote.

questions.question: Every citizen above eighteen is ___ to vote provided he/she is duly registered.
questions.explanation: **Eligible** means having the right or entitlement.

questions.question: recommended (This item appears to be an option from an unstated question, options are below)
questions.explanation: This item is a repetition or fragmentation of previous questions/options and does not form a complete, unique question.

questions.question: booths (This item appears to be an option from an unstated question, options are below)
questions.explanation: This item is a repetition or fragmentation of previous questions/options and does not form a complete, unique question.

questions.question: endorsement (This item appears to be an option from an unstated question, options are below)
questions.explanation: This item is a repetition or fragmentation of previous questions/options and does not form a complete, unique question.
